name: I slipped on ice at work in Salt Lake City and hurt my back bad, what do I do right now?
description: Report to employer immediately, get medical help, take photos, and call a workers' comp lawyer—Utah requires prompt notice for claims.[1]

mainEntity.name: I slipped on ice at work in Salt Lake City and hurt my back bad, what do I do right now?

acceptedAnswer.text: Report to employer immediately, get medical help, take photos, and call a workers' comp lawyer—Utah requires prompt notice for claims.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: **Immediately report the injury to your employer and seek medical attention.** In Utah workplaces, workers' compensation covers slip-and-fall injuries regardless of fault, but you must notify your employer promptly to preserve your claim.[1][2] Key factors include documenting the scene with photos, getting witness statements, and avoiding any admission of fault to HR. Follow up with a doctor's visit for a full evaluation, as delayed symptoms can complicate claims. Consult a workers' comp attorney to file properly and avoid denials. This ensures you get medical bills paid and lost wages.
